1.name: Everyday Chit-Chat
1.subtitle: Daily habits & lifestyle
1.description: Establish basic psychological safety and observe each other's daily rhythms and micro-habits.

questions.text: If you had to eat the exact same thing for breakfast every day without getting sick of it, what specific food would you choose?
Describe your typical "wind-down ritual" in the last 30 minutes before you go to sleep.
When you take a shower, what is the very first body part you wash?
When you wake up, what is the very first app you open on your phone?
Name one ingredient that absolutely does not belong on a pizza (or in your favorite dish), and briefly explain why.
What is your most common habit or activity when riding public transportation or commuting?
If you open your closet right now, what specific color or style of clothing takes up the most space?
With an unlimited budget, what does your absolute dream living environment look like (e.g., a beachfront cabin, a high-rise penthouse)?
What was the last item you bought as a pure "impulse purchase"?
If you were forced to work out, what specific physical activity would you dread the least?
When you go to a coffee shop or cafe, what is your exact, highly specific go-to order?
How often do you do a deep, thorough clean of your bedroom or apartment?
In a group trip, what specific role do you unconsciously take on (e.g., the human GPS, the foodie, the one who sleeps in)?
How many unread notifications do you currently have on your messaging app?
What specific type of weather or temperature instantly puts you in a good mood?
When traveling far from home, what specific "non-essential, but comforting" item must you pack in your suitcase?
If you had to cook your signature dish to host friends today, what specific meal would you make?
When playing video games or board games, what specific quirky habit do you have (e.g., exploring every corner of the map, hoarding items, or rushing the main quest)?
If you could adopt any pet right now (ignoring space, money, or climate constraints), what specific animal would you choose?
If you walk into a convenience store or gas station just to grab a quick craving, what is your go-to snack or drink?
Within the first hour of waking up, what is the one specific thing you must do, otherwise your day hasn't "truly started"?
What specific, non-practical item must you have on your desk or workspace just to make yourself happy?
When shopping for clothes, shoes, or bags, what specific "design detail" or "material" inexplicably draws you in?
If you could only keep one non-essential "quality-of-life" small appliance in your home, which one would it be?
When walking alone down the street, what specific thing usually catches your eye (e.g., typography on signs, people's outfits, stray animals)?
When you stroll through a night market, what is the one snack you almost always buy?
At your go-to local breakfast shop, what is your usual fixed order?
When it's pouring rain and you have to go out, what is your standard gear or survival strategy?
At a buffet or bento shop, what is the one side dish you always grab?
When a surprise day off is announced (like a typhoon day), how do you usually spend it?
What is your one true go-to hand-shaken drink order?
When summer gets unbearably hot and humid, what is your most relied-upon way to cool down?
At a big family reunion dinner (like Lunar New Year's Eve), which dish do you look forward to the most?
When you suddenly get hungry late at night, what is your go-to midnight snack?
During the Mid-Autumn Festival, what is the one activity that always happens with your family or friends?
If your bedroom could permanently smell like one specific scent, what would you choose?
When taking a group photo, what is your go-to signature pose or hand gesture?
When washing your hands or face, what is your specific water temperature preference?
What specific type of cup or vessel do you most often use to drink water at home or in the office?
Looking at your shoe closet, which specific pair of shoes is your most worn and heavily used?
How do you typically handle your receipts after buying something?
How many alarms do you typically have to set to ensure you wake up on a workday morning?
What is the one absolute "must-have" dipping sauce or condiment when you eat dumplings, hot pot, or savory pancakes?
When watching series or videos, what playback speed do you typically use, or what are your fast-forwarding habits?
What is the one non-negotiable rule or detail you always stick to regarding your current hairstyle?
When adding toppings to your bubble tea, what is your absolute first-choice topping?
How do you typically handle or store it when you accumulate a pile of loose change in your wallet or pocket?
What is your most frequently purchased, all-time favorite microwavable meal at the convenience store?
What specific, non-sleeping item is currently permanently sitting on your bedside table or right next to your pillow?
On which specific day of the week or time of day do you typically do your laundry?
When turning on the air conditioner at home during summer, what specific temperature setting is your go-to?
After washing your hair, do you blow-dry it immediately or do you tend to delay it for a while?
Aside from cash and cards, what specific "clutter" most commonly piles up in your wallet?
How do you typically apply ketchup when eating french fries?
What specific moment in your daily routine is the one where you absolutely must put on headphones?

2.name: Happy Hour / Pub Talk
2.subtitle: Personal flair & social preferences
2.description: Showcase unique tastes, project interests, and share views on the outside world.

questions.text: With an unlimited budget and a full week off, which country would you most want to visit?
What do you think is the most common first impression people have of you?
When your social battery runs out at a large gathering, what specific excuse or method do you use to slip away?
What is a movie you love so much that you've watched it more than three times?
Aside from the paycheck, what specific aspect of your job gives you the most sense of accomplishment?
Which public figure or fictional character do you admire the most?
What is the most successful, surprising gift you've ever given someone that you are still proud of?
When you're feeling mildly stressed, what specific food instantly comforts you?
If you could instantly master a new language or musical instrument, which one would you choose?
In a social setting, what specific behavior instantly makes you lose respect for someone?
What does your perfect Saturday afternoon look like?
If you had to describe yourself as an animal, what would you be?
If your house were on fire and you could only save one non-electronic personal item, what would it be?
When picking a restaurant for a group dinner, what specific type of place or "red flag" would make you absolutely avoid it?
Which social media platform do you use the most frequently?
If an AI robot could take over exactly one daily chore you despise, what specific task would you assign it?
If you were to start a completely unprofitable, niche "side hustle" or hobby, what would it be?
If you could live inside the universe of any video game, movie, or anime for a month, which specific world would you choose?
In a group hangout, if the conversation suddenly goes dead silent, what specific topic or action do you use to save the vibe?
If you found $50 on the street and had to blow it purely on entertainment today, what exactly would you do with it?
If you were forced to start a YouTube channel or Podcast today, what specific topic would you cover?
If you could get free, front-row VIP tickets to any live event (concert, theater, sports), who or what would you go see?
In the past year, what is the smallest, most trivial achievement that you felt secretly proud of for a long time?
When browsing the internet, what specific type of clickbait title or video genre always gets you to click and go down a rabbit hole?
Name a specific song or soundtrack that instantly teleports you back to a specific memory from your school days.
If you could take a foreign friend to eat one food that best represents Taiwan, what would it be?
Which spot in Taiwan (main island or offshore) is your hidden gem that you'd love to revisit?
If you had to move away from where you live now, which city or county in Taiwan would you choose?
Which Taiwanese drama or film would you most recommend to a friend?
At karaoke (KTV), what is your "safe bet" go-to song?
Name one childhood snack or old-school treat you still can't resist buying today?
Which TV show did you never miss back when you were a student?
What specific food would you willingly queue more than 30 minutes for?
Among night market game stalls (balloon darts, goldfish scooping, pinball...), which one are you most confident at?
What is your ideal way to spend the New Year's Eve countdown?
When taking photos or posting stories, what specific style of filter do you prefer?
Which specific song have you had on repeat the most over the past month?
If you were to gift a book you genuinely love to a friend, which one would you choose?
If you could have dinner with any historical figure who is no longer living, who would you invite?
What was the very first music album or single you bought with your own money?
What is the most unusual or useless thing you have ever collected?
In your mind, what must a "luxurious and perfect" weekend breakfast include?
What is your tolerance level for horror movies, and what specific type of horror element scares you the most?
When playing board games with friends, what specific type of game do you enjoy the most?
What popular tourist attraction did you visit that you found to be completely overrated?
If you could have one "practical everyday" superpower, what would it be?
What was the single most memorable and high-energy live concert you have ever attended?
If your phone ran out of space, which app that you installed but rarely open would be the first one you uninstall?
Which specific accessory or clothing item in your daily outfit makes you feel "this is uniquely my style"?
What is the single most important feature or vibe that your ideal cafe must have?
Which natural landscape in Taiwan do you think is the most beautiful and worth recommending to foreigners?
What is the one Taiwanese Hokkien phrase you find yourself using most naturally in daily life?
What is the one anime or cartoon you absolutely had to rush home to watch after school when you were a kid?
If you were to participate in a road run, what unusual or specialty food would you most hope to find at the supply station?
What is your favorite completely brainless hobby or entertainment to wind down after work or school?

3.name: Deep Conversations
3.subtitle: Emotional resonance & life complaints
3.description: Lower defenses, share emotional experiences, and reveal inner turbulence.

questions.text: What was the last movie or book that actually made you cry?
What specific behavior from a boss or coworker is absolutely intolerable to you?
When looking for a partner, what specific trait or core value do you prioritize above all else?
If you could turn back time, which specific stage of your life would you return to and make a different choice?
When you are feeling anxious, who is the very first person you want to call for comfort?
What specific action by a friend would make you cut ties with them completely?
Growing up, what specific trait of yours were you the most insecure about?
What is a specific small detail—something your partner did or said—that made you feel profoundly loved?
What was the single most regretful job you’ve ever taken?
When you are extremely angry, what specific behavior or reaction do you typically display?
Describe a time you pretended to like something just to fit in or please a group.
Which specific aspect of your parents' personalities has had the deepest impact on you?
When you think of your first love, what is the very first keyword that pops into your head?
When you are completely exhausted, what is the core motivation that keeps you pushing forward?
What is the most "out-of-your-comfort-zone" activity you have ever done completely by yourself (e.g., going to a concert, eating at a fancy sit-down restaurant)?
What recent, specific life event gave you a deep sense of powerlessness or almost made you "snap"?
Looking back at your past relationships, what specific kind of person (or specific individual) do you feel you owe a sincere apology to?
In an intimate relationship (partner or best friend), what kind of "trivial everyday thing" most easily makes you secretly sulk?
Seeing people around you achieve what "specific milestone or status" most easily triggers your own anxiety?
After experiencing a terrible setback, what "specific action" do you want friends to take to support you (instead of just saying "cheer up")?
What is the most common misconception people have about your personality, which is entirely untrue on the inside?
Looking back, what seemingly insignificant "tiny decision" ended up drastically altering the trajectory of your life?
What is the biggest "break of your own rules" or compromise you’ve ever made for a friend or partner?
When dealing with "loss" (a relationship, an environment, or an object), what specific ritual do you need to go through to truly move on?
Name a slightly "dark" or "arrogant" trait in your personality that you actually kind of like about yourself.
At family gatherings, which classic line of "concern" from elders makes you roll your eyes inside?
What is one thing in everyday life that "everyone treats as normal" but quietly fills you with helplessness?
Growing up, who were you most often compared to by your parents or elders?
What is the one unwritten rule of workplace culture you most want to change?
When living away from home (for school, work, or long travel), what specific thing from home do you miss the most?
When someone important leaves you on read, what little drama usually plays out in your head?
What is the one thing you most regret NOT doing back in your school days?
Think of a friendship that slowly faded — what do you think was the real reason it faded?
Which "it's for your own good" line puts the most pressure on you?
During the big holiday season (like Lunar New Year), which part exhausts you the most?
When you earned your very first paycheck or job money, what did you buy for yourself or your family?
What specific type of social gathering do you dread or dislike attending the most?
When a friend is crying or upset, what is your go-to sentence or action to comfort them?
What is the most common reason or positive motivation behind your occasional white lies in daily life?
When you begin to feel annoyed or angry, what subtle "warning sign" do you show that alerts others?
What was the single most decisive factor that made you decide to leave your last job or change your career path?
Is there a specific text message or conversation on your phone that you cherish and repeatedly read?
If someone dislikes you, what specific trait or habit of yours do you think is usually the reason?
If you could talk to your self from ten years ago for one minute, what specific advice would you give?
When arguing with someone close, what is your typical communication or coping style?
Once someone breaks your trust, is the relationship restorable? If so, what must they do?
Ignoring climate and career constraints, which town or district in Taiwan would you most want to retire in?
What is the most painful criticism you've ever received, which you later admitted was actually very true?
What specific moment in your life made you suddenly realize, "I am truly an adult now"?
How long does it typically take you to truly and completely let go of a relationship that has ended?
Name a personal ritual in your life that you insist on keeping, even if others might find it tedious or unnecessary.
How do you typically react or feel inside when someone praises you warmly to your face?
In a group activity, what boundary being crossed would make you refuse to cooperate, regardless of saving face?
What specific scent most strongly triggers your childhood memories?
What specific situation in a friendship or romance makes you feel the most passive or hesitant to take initiative?

4.name: Late Night Confessions
4.subtitle: Secrets, hidden worries & true vulnerability
4.description: Deep disclosure, touching upon the vulnerable self and hidden truths.

questions.text: What is the deepest fear hidden in your heart that you are always avoiding facing?
Share a tiny secret you’ve buried in your heart for a long time and never confessed to your friends.
Write down a specific quirk or habit you have that nobody in this room knows about.
If you were to pass away tomorrow, who is the one person you most want to say "I'm sorry" to right now?
What is the one flaw in your own personality that you hate the most?
What kind of facade do you typically use to protect yourself when around strangers?
What is the craziest, most unconventional thing you have ever done in your life?
In interpersonal relationships, what specific event or behavior instantly makes you build deep trust with someone?
What is a dream you were forced to abandon for practical reasons, but still long for?
When was the last time you felt completely out of place and disconnected from the world?
Confess the most selfish thing you have ever done but never told anyone about.
What specific childhood experience still impacts your adult life today?
When you are completely burnt out from socializing and just want to disappear, where do you go or what do you do to heal yourself?
Setting fame and wealth aside, what does true "success" look like in your heart?
Honestly speaking, what specific achievement or possession of a close friend has ever caused you to feel a brief flash of jealousy?
What real situation do you most often hide from your family?
What category of search keywords have you secretly looked up late at night that you'd never want anyone to see?
In which recurring setting (office, family gatherings, reunions...) do you wear your thickest mask?
What money situation or spending habit do you have that even your closest people don't know about?
The last time you cried in secret behind everyone's back, what was it about?
What excuse do you most often use to cover up the fact that you simply don't want to socialize?
Share one sentence you once blurted out that still makes you feel guilty today.
Which achievement or advantage that others envy do you secretly feel you don't deserve?
What is the one sentence to your family that you've kept buried and never managed to say?
Which part of your current life are you "pretending to like" when it's actually a compromise?
In your view, what is the difference between solitude and loneliness?
When you are alone late at night, what specific ability or decision of yours do you doubt the most?
What specific vulnerable state or emotion do you dread showing the people closest to you?
What was the biggest compromise you made during your youth to satisfy your parents' or society's expectations?
In your past relationships, which connection fading away still hurts and feels irrecoverable?
Honestly speaking, what specific savings amount do you need to accumulate to feel true financial security?
When thinking about getting older, what specific ability or physical state are you most afraid of losing?
When under extreme mental pressure and choosing to avoid it, what is your go-to method of escape?
Is there something someone did to you that you said was okay, but in your heart you have never truly forgiven?
In the past six months, what specific event caused you major grievance that you chose to keep completely to yourself?
What is the single most extreme act of solitude you have ever done in your life?
Honestly, what specific personality trait of a close friend do you envy the most?
What is the biggest cost you pay to maintain your outward image of being cheerful, reliable, or gentle?
If you could never meet again, what final blessing or message would you send to someone who has drifted out of your life?
What private bias do you hold against a certain type of people that you know is logically wrong but still feel emotionally?
When you have insomnia, what specific thought about the meaninglessness of life hits your brain?
What was the most passive decision you've ever made where you gave up on a romance or dream without even trying?
When was the last time you felt deeply disappointed in your own behavior or choices, and why?
Deep down, what specific apology do you most want to say to your childhood self?
What specific moment in a relationship makes you feel utterly lonely even when you are with your partner?
When you are completely broken down, what specific environment or space do you need to hide in to start healing?

5.name: Soul Interrogation
5.subtitle: Core values & extreme challenges
5.description: Challenge boundaries, explore the meaning of life, and test moral limits.

questions.text: If you had to trade the most important thing in your life (tangible or intangible) for absolute freedom, what would you trade away?
If the world were ending and you could only bring one person onto Noah's Ark, what cruel criteria would you use to choose that person?
If someone offered you ten million dollars on the condition that they erase one specific memory from your life, which memory would you choose to erase?
If you knew for a fact you only had one year left to live, exactly how would you plan your next 365 days?
If you gained the power of immortality, what "ultimate life mission" would you set for yourself?
Name a specific action that you believe is "morally or legally wrong, but under certain circumstances, you would absolutely do it."
If you had to reintroduce "who you are" to a completely amnesiac version of yourself, what would be the three most important sentences you write down?
Name a specific setback that caused you immense pain, but looking back now, you are glad it happened.
If your sacrifice could buy one specific change for the world, what exact change would you want your life to be traded for?
Under what extreme circumstances would you choose to betray your most cherished principles?
If the world is doomed to be destroyed in fifty years, what final message or advice would you leave for today's younger generation?
Describe the specific moment in your life where you felt the most "ultimate, absolute loneliness."
Name a specific action that, even if done by your closest friend or family member, you could absolutely never forgive.
If you could trade ten years of your life for one supernatural ability, what exact power would you choose?
If you had to engrave a short phrase summarizing your entire life on your tombstone, what would you engrave?
If you could carry only one memory into your next life, which one would you choose?
If you could learn one absolute truth about yourself (how people really see you, the date of your death...), what would you choose to know?
If your life had to completely restart but you could keep one existing thing (a relationship, an ability, a memory...), what would you keep?
Which one of your moral lines do you believe can never be bought, no matter the price offered?
If you could anonymously disappear for one year with zero consequences, what kind of life would you live?
If you had to defend the worst thing you have ever done, what would you say?
For the person you love most, what is the one thing you absolutely could NOT do?
If you could ask a deity one question with a guaranteed true answer, what would you ask?
If you could leave behind only one object for the most important person after you're gone, what would it be?
If you could permanently erase one form of human suffering from the world (hunger, loneliness, illness, betrayal...), which would you erase?
If selling the most precious trait of your soul (e.g., kindness) could guarantee a lifetime of health for your loved one, would you sell it?
In an extreme survival situation where you must choose between betraying a friend and self-sacrifice, which does your instinct choose?
Which is closer to hell for you: living with painful memories or living happily with no memories at all?
If you could watch a video of the end of your life but were unable to change any of it, would you choose to watch?
If someone who hurt you deeply apologizes sincerely years later, is it more cruel to force yourself to forgive or to age holding onto resentment?
If you had the power to make one human invention (e.g., money, weapons, the internet) permanently vanish from history, what would you erase?
Would you be willing to sacrifice your most important current friendship or family relationship to find your lifetime soulmate?
If given the chance to completely reshape your personality, would you give up your current self-awareness to become a perfect person?
If you had to stand trial before an omniscient higher being after death, what sin of your life would be the hardest to defend?
If saving your country or the world required sacrificing the life of someone you love, would you choose the few loved ones or the many strangers?
If you could choose to freeze your physiological aging at a specific age but could never have biological children, at what age would you freeze?
If you discovered your happiest memory was actually a massive lie, would you choose to expose it or continue living in ignorance?
If the world was doomed to endless suffering and only your eternal torment could end it, would you accept that fate?
At what level of pain or desperation do you think absolute human selfishness becomes entirely forgivable?
If your consciousness could be uploaded for digital immortality after death but you lost all physical sensory pleasure, would you upload?
If the apocalypse was coming and you could post one final message online for all of humanity to see, what would you write?
If you could live forever in a 100% happy but virtual Matrix-like world, would you choose to never wake up?
If you could gain the highest intellect or creativity but lost the ability to feel loved or to love, would you make the trade?
If you could explore the most beautiful unknown planet in the universe but could never return to Earth or see anyone again, would you go?
If you stripped away your job, social status, family identity, and all possessions, what is the core essence of your soul that keeps you alive?
